title: cordia truncatifolia
intro: Cordia truncatifolia, also known as the Wild Plum or Dwarf Plum, is a deciduous shrub native to India and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cordia truncatifolia include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and combat infections. Cordia truncatifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, or used as a poultice or topical treatment to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
